start.vercelSignin: Jolly runs the Vercel sign-in (`vercel login`) with you up front, before the unattended stages.
start.vercelSigninIncomplete: Vercel sign-in didn't complete — no session was established. The deploy stage will surface a fresh sign-in link you can approve; the rest of the setup continues.
start.vercelSignin.failSpawn: Vercel sign-in didn't complete — Jolly could not launch the Vercel CLI at all, so no sign-in link could be produced. Check that `npx vercel whoami` runs. The deploy stage will offer a fresh link; the rest of the setup continues.
start.vercelSignin.noOutput: (the Vercel CLI produced no output at all — it could not be launched, e.g. no network route to install or run it)
start.vercelSignin.failNotApproved: Vercel sign-in didn't complete — the sign-in link was never approved in the browser, so no session was established. Full output: {logPath}. The deploy stage will offer a fresh link; the rest of the setup continues.
start.note.vercelSignin: Open this link and approve the Vercel sign-in
start.vercelSigninWaiting: Waiting for you to approve the Vercel sign-in in your browser…
start.vercelSigninDone: Vercel sign-in approved — signed in as {account}.
start.vercelSigninTimeout: Vercel sign-in not approved in time.
start.stripeFinal: Last step is yours, once Jolly's done: paste the Stripe keys in the Saleor Dashboard.
start.proceed: Build your store now? This creates the store, storefront, and deployment.
start.declined: Stopped before building anything. Nothing was created.
start.reusingConfiguredStore: Reusing your already-configured store ({storeUrl}); resuming the remaining stages.
start.previewedPlan: Previewed the plan. No files were written and no changes were made.
start.prompt.organization: Choose the Saleor organization to use
start.prompt.store: Create a new store, or reuse an existing one?
start.close.notFinished: Setup did not finish — the {stages} {stageWord} did not complete{reasons}.
start.close.reRun: Re-run `jolly start` to finish the remaining stages.
start.close.keepStorefront: storefront/ — your storefront, live on Vercel (pnpm dev, npx vercel)
start.close.keepRecipe: recipe.yml — your catalog & store config as code (npx @saleor/configurator)
saleorUrl.clarification: That doesn't look like a Saleor URL I can use. Could you paste your Saleor Dashboard URL, GraphQL API URL, or root Saleor Cloud URL (for example https://your-store.eu.saleor.cloud)?
login.summary.emptyToken: JOLLY_SALEOR_CLOUD_TOKEN is set but empty. Nothing was written.
login.error.emptyToken.message: JOLLY_SALEOR_CLOUD_TOKEN is set to an empty value; no token was read.
login.error.emptyToken.remediation: JOLLY_SALEOR_CLOUD_TOKEN is empty. Unset it, then run `jolly login` to sign in through the Saleor device authorization grant.
login.next.runLogin: Run `jolly login` to sign in through the Saleor device authorization grant.
login.next.runLoginOrSetToken: Run `jolly login` to sign in through the Saleor device authorization grant, or set JOLLY_SALEOR_CLOUD_TOKEN for non-interactive use.
login.summary.rejected: The token was rejected by the Cloud API. Nothing was written.
login.error.invalidToken.message: Saleor Cloud rejected the token (HTTP 401/403). It was not stored.
login.error.invalidToken.remediation: Run `jolly login` to sign in through the Saleor device authorization grant.
login.check.cloudTokenVerification.fail: Token rejected by the Cloud API.
login.next.runLoginAfterRejection: Run `jolly login` to sign in through the Saleor device authorization grant.
login.summary.storedNotVerified: Token stored, not verified — the Cloud API was unreachable.
login.check.cloudTokenVerification.unkno: stored, not verified — the Cloud API was unreachable.
login.next.reRunWhenReachable: Re-run jolly login when the Cloud API is reachable to verify the token.
login.summary.storedWithOrganization: Token verified and stored. Authenticated as "{orgName}".
login.check.cloudTokenVerification.pass: Token verified against the Cloud API organizations endpoint.
login.next.createStore: Run jolly create store to provision a Saleor Cloud environment.
login.summary.success: Signed in through the Saleor device authorization grant.
login.next: Hand this link to your human to approve the Saleor sign-in in their browser — it's their account, not yours: {url}. Tell them to reply "done" once they've approved; then re-run `{command}` and I continue.
login.summary.approvalPending: Approve the Saleor sign-in in your browser, then re-run `jolly login` to finish.
logout.summary.removed: Removed Jolly-managed Saleor auth values from .env ({removed}).
logout.summary.nothingPresent: No Jolly-managed Saleor auth values were present in .env.
logout.check.authCleared.pass: Jolly-managed Saleor auth values are no longer in .env.
logout.next: Run jolly login to authenticate again when needed.
auth.status.summary.configured: Saleor Cloud authentication is configured (account context: {accountContext}).
auth.status.summary.notConfigured: Saleor Cloud authentication is not configured.
auth.status.next: Run jolly login to configure Saleor Cloud authentication.
createStore.summary.urlNotNormalizable: The provided URL could not be normalized to a Saleor GraphQL endpoint.
createStore.error.invalidSaleorUrl.remed: Paste a Saleor Dashboard, GraphQL, or root Saleor Cloud URL.
createStore.next.reRunWithSaleorUrl: Re-run with a Saleor Dashboard, GraphQL, or root Saleor Cloud URL.
createStore.summary.nonFirstPartyHost: Refusing to use a non-first-party host: {pastedHost}.
createStore.error.nonFirstPartyHost.mess: Refusing to send a request to non-first-party host {pastedHost}.
createStore.error.nonFirstPartyHost.reme: Use your Saleor Cloud store URL (a *.saleor.cloud endpoint).
createStore.next.reRunWithCloudUrl: Re-run with your Saleor Cloud store URL.
createStore.summary.previewedOnly: Previewed storing the Saleor endpoint; nothing was written.
createStore.next.reRunWithoutDryRun: Run the command without --dry-run to write the endpoint to .env.
createStore.summary.endpointConflict: A different NEXT_PUBLIC_SALEOR_API_URL already exists in .env; Jolly paused instead of overwriting it. Re-run with --yes to replace it.
createStore.check.saleorEndpointCollisio: An existing NEXT_PUBLIC_SALEOR_API_URL would be overwritten; not replaced without --yes.
createStore.next.reRunWithYesToOverwrite: Re-run with --yes to overwrite the existing endpoint (the agent decides).
createStore.summary.storedWithOrganizati: Wrote NEXT_PUBLIC_SALEOR_API_URL to .env; resolved organization "{organization}" from the endpoint host.
createStore.summary.storedNotVerified: Wrote NEXT_PUBLIC_SALEOR_API_URL to .env; the endpoint is stored, not verified.
createStore.check.saleorEndpointStored.p: NEXT_PUBLIC_SALEOR_API_URL written to .env; the endpoint is stored, not verified.
createStore.next.runStartToContinue: Run jolly start to continue the end-to-end setup; it recognizes the stored store and resumes rather than redoing it.
createStore.summary.noToken: No Saleor Cloud token is configured; cannot provision a store.
createStore.error.missingCloudToken.mess: JOLLY_SALEOR_CLOUD_TOKEN is required to create a Saleor Cloud store.
createStore.next.runLoginForToken: Run jolly login to acquire a Saleor Cloud token.
createStore.summary.noOrganizations: The Cloud token has access to no organizations.
createStore.error.noOrganizations.messag: No organizations are accessible with this Cloud token.
createStore.error.noOrganizations.remedi: Run `jolly login` to sign in through the Saleor device authorization grant, then retry.
createStore.next.signInWithOrganization: Sign in with an account that has a Saleor Cloud organization, then retry.
createStore.env.summary.warning: Previewed environment creation in "{selectedOrg}" (token has multiple organizations).
createStore.env.next.reRunWithoutDryRun: Run the command without --dry-run to create the environment.
createStore.summary.multipleOrganization: The Cloud token has multiple organizations; Jolly selected "{selectedOrg}".
Selected "{selectedOrg}". Re-run with --organization <slug> if this is wrong.
createStore.next.reRunWithOrganization: Re-run with --organization <slug> to choose explicitly. Available: {organizations}.
createStore.summary.environmentUnreachab: Saleor Cloud environment "{environmentName}" created in "{selectedOrg}", but its endpoint did not become reachable within the readiness budget.
createStore.check.environmentProvisioned: Environment created, but its Saleor endpoint did not become reachable within the readiness budget.
Environment created and verified via task status.
createStore.remediation: The store may still be starting up. Re-run in a few moments to confirm.
createStore.next.reRunDoctorWhenServing: The store may still be starting up. Re-run the doctor checks in a few moments to confirm the endpoint serves.
createStore.next.continueBootstrap: Continue the bootstrap against the new store.
cloudError.summary.error: The Cloud API request failed. Nothing was created.
cloudError.next.freeEnvironment: Free a sandbox environment by deleting an unused one, then re-run.
cloudError.next.upgradePlan: Upgrade the plan to raise the sandbox environment limit, then re-run.
create.help.summary.success: jolly create exposes the plumbing subcommand store.
create.help.next.store: Provision a Saleor Cloud store/environment, or store a pasted Saleor URL.
create.help.next.createEnvironment: Run jolly create store --create-environment to provision a Saleor Cloud environment.
create.error.unknownCreateSubcommand.mes: "{sub}" is not a create subcommand. Valid: {subcommands}.
create.error.unknownCreateSubcommand.rem: Run `jolly create --help` to list available subcommands.
init.check.skill.unverified: {id} could not be verified on disk after npx skills add.{stderr}
init.check.agentsMd.pass: Merged the Jolly section into AGENTS.md.
init.summary.error: Some skills could not be verified on disk: {failures}.
init.error.skillInstallFailed.remediatio: Ensure `npx skills` is available and the network is reachable, then re-run `jolly init`.
init.next.checkNetwork: Check the network is reachable, then re-run the install to verify the skills on disk.
init.summary.success: Installed and verified {length} skills; merged .mcp.json and AGENTS.md.
init.next.restartAgent: Restart your agent now, before continuing — the skills just installed only load into a fresh session, so without a restart they stay inactive and Jolly's guidance drifts out of your context. Tell your human to restart you, then resume.
init.next.runStart: Run jolly start to bootstrap setup and get the ordered playbook.
doctor.error.unknownDoctorGroup.message: "{group}" is not a doctor group. Valid: {groups}.
doctor.error.unknownDoctorGroup.remediat: Run `jolly doctor` for all checks or name a valid group.
doctor.next: Run all the checks, or name one of: {groups}.
doctor.check.mcpConfig.fail: No .mcp.json with a saleor-graphql entry; run jolly init to merge it.
doctor.check.agentsMd.pass: AGENTS.md carries the Jolly marker section.
doctor.check.agentsMd.fail: AGENTS.md is missing or lacks the Jolly marker section; run jolly init to merge it.
doctor.check.saleorCloudToken.storeToken: JOLLY_SALEOR_CLOUD_TOKEN looks like a store access token, not a Cloud staff token. Run `jolly login` to sign in through the Saleor device authorization grant.
doctor.check.saleorCloudToken.pass: Cloud token authenticated a read-only GET of {orgEndpoint}; organization "{slug}".
doctor.check.saleorCloudToken.noOrganiza: Cloud token authenticated {orgEndpoint} but it returned no organizations. Run `jolly login` to sign in through the Saleor device authorization grant.
doctor.check.saleorCloudToken.rejected: Cloud token was rejected: the read-only GET of {orgEndpoint} returned HTTP {httpStatus}. Run `jolly login` to sign in through the Saleor device authorization grant.
doctor.check.saleorCloudToken.unknown: Could not reach the Cloud API organizations endpoint ({orgEndpoint}) to verify the token in this run.
doctor.check.saleorEndpoint.pass: NEXT_PUBLIC_SALEOR_API_URL is reachable and responds as a GraphQL endpoint.
doctor.check.saleorEndpoint.unknown: NEXT_PUBLIC_SALEOR_API_URL is set but live connectivity could not be verified in this run.
doctor.check.saleorToken.pass: SALEOR_TOKEN is configured for store GraphQL (sent Bearer).
doctor.check.saleorToken.fail: No SALEOR_TOKEN configured for store GraphQL.
doctor.check.usChannelPurchasable.skippe: Store endpoint or SALEOR_TOKEN not configured; `us`-channel purchasability was not checked.
doctor.check.usChannelPurchasable.pass: The `us` channel offers {count} product(s) available for purchase.
doctor.check.usChannelPurchasable.warnin: The store is reachable but no product is available for purchase in the `us` channel — add channel listings (with price + availability) so products can sell; checkout otherwise fails before payment.
doctor.check.usChannelPurchasable.unknow: Could not reach the store GraphQL to verify `us`-channel purchasability in this run.
doctor.check.pnpmAvailable.global: pnpm is available ({version}); the storefront stage installs Paper's dependencies with it.
doctor.check.pnpmAvailable.viaNpx: No global pnpm; the storefront stage runs `npx pnpm@latest install` (no global pnpm install required).
doctor.check.storefrontPresent.unknown: A project structure exists; Paper storefront readiness not verified in this run.
doctor.check.storefrontPresent.fail: No Paper storefront detected locally. `jolly start` clones and prepares the Paper storefront.
doctor.check.deploymentStatus.skipped: Deployment is run by your agent via the Vercel CLI; Jolly does not contact Vercel.
doctor.check.vercelAuth.signedIn: Vercel CLI session confirmed by running `vercel whoami`: logged in as {account}.
doctor.check.vercelAuth.notSignedIn: No Vercel CLI session: `vercel whoami` reported you are not logged in.
doctor.check.checkoutPaymentGateway.skip: Checkout-readiness probe skipped: no Saleor endpoint and/or token to reach the store. Once the store is reachable, this probe creates a reverted `us` test checkout to confirm the Stripe gateway is offered.
doctor.check.checkoutPaymentGateway.pass: Checkout is ready: a `us` checkout is offered the Stripe payment gateway, so checkout can progress to the Stripe test payment step.
doctor.check.checkoutPaymentGateway.warn: The store is reachable but a `us` checkout is not yet offered the Stripe gateway. Complete the remaining keys + `us`-channel Dashboard step:
doctor.check.checkoutPaymentGateway.noBu: Checkout-readiness could not be determined: a `us` test checkout could not be created (no buyable variant or no `us` channel). Seed stock and deploy the starter recipe, then re-run.
doctor.check.checkoutPaymentGateway.endp: Checkout-readiness probe could not reach the store's Saleor GraphQL endpoint (NEXT_PUBLIC_SALEOR_API_URL); checkout readiness was not verified.
doctor.summary.failed: Some checks failed; see next steps.
doctor.error.doctorChecksFailed.remediat: Address the failing checks listed in nextSteps.
skills.next.installMissing: Run jolly init (or jolly start) to install the missing skills.
skills.summary.managed: Jolly manages {length} skills (install via npx skills add).
skills.next.installSet: Run jolly init (or jolly start) to install the skill set.
upgrade.check.paperBaseline.unknown: Paper storefront detected (baseline version {version}); Jolly plans Paper migrations but does not auto-apply them in v1.
upgrade.check.paperBaseline.skipped: No Paper storefront detected; nothing to plan.
upgrade.summary.success: Checked Jolly-managed skills and guidance for updates; Paper changes are plan-only.

start.check.startDryRun.skipped: This is a dry-run preview; no stage was executed.

start.store.check.storeProvisioned.reuse: A Saleor endpoint is already configured; reusing it.
start.store.check.storeProvisioned.skipp: Cannot provision a store: no JOLLY_SALEOR_CLOUD_TOKEN configured (complete login first).
start.store.check.storeProvisioned.noOrg: The Cloud token has access to no organizations.
start.store.check.storeProvisioned.unrea: Provisioned Saleor Cloud environment "{environmentName}" in "{selectedOrg}", but its Saleor endpoint did not become reachable within the readiness budget.
The store may still be starting up. Re-run jolly start --yes in a few moments.
start.recipe.check.recipeDeployed.skippe: Cannot deploy the starter recipe: NEXT_PUBLIC_SALEOR_API_URL and/or SALEOR_TOKEN are not configured.
Complete the store stage so the endpoint and SALEOR_TOKEN are in .env, then re-run jolly start --yes.
start.recipe.check.recipeDeployed.bundle: Cannot deploy the starter recipe: bundled recipe not found at {bundledRecipe}.
Reinstall jolly so the bundled assets/skills/jolly/recipe.yml is present.
start.recipe.check.recipeDeployed.writeF: Cannot deploy the starter recipe: could not write it to {recipePath}: {error}.
Ensure the project directory is writable, then re-run jolly start --yes.
start.recipe.check.recipeDeployed.notDep: Did not deploy the starter recipe: {reason}.
Verify npx can reach @saleor/configurator and NEXT_PUBLIC_SALEOR_API_URL/SALEOR_TOKEN reach the store, then re-run jolly start --yes.
start.recipe.check.recipeDeployed.collec: The configurator reported the recipe deploy succeeded, but the declared featured collection could not be confirmed populated in the store.
start.recipe.check.recipeDeployed.pass: Deployed the starter recipe via @saleor/configurator deploy; the declared featured collection was confirmed populated in the store.
start.recipe.remediation: See the recipe-collections check; re-run jolly start --yes once the catalog deploy is complete so the declared products exist.
start.recipe.check.recipeDeployed.destru: Did not deploy the starter recipe: the configurator detected deletions over a pre-existing store (blocked by --failOnDelete).{stderr}
Review the destructive diff. Deploying over an existing catalog requires the customer's explicit approval; the happy path is the blank store jolly start itself provisions.
start.recipe.check.recipeDeployed.config: Did not deploy the starter recipe: @saleor/configurator deploy exited {status}{reportStatus}.{stderr}
Verify NEXT_PUBLIC_SALEOR_API_URL and SALEOR_TOKEN reach the store, then re-run jolly start --yes.
start.recipe.collections.check.productsA: Populated {assigned} of {declared} declared product(s) into the recipe's featured collection(s); {remaining} declared product(s) were absent from the store.
Re-run jolly start --yes once the catalog deploy is complete so every declared product exists to assign.
start.recipe.collections.check.recipeCol: Assigned {assigned} of {declared} declared product(s) to the recipe's featured collection(s) via collectionAddProducts (the configurator cannot populate collection membership in a single deploy).
start.recipe.collections.check.populateF: Deployed the starter recipe but could not populate its featured collection: {error}.
Verify NEXT_PUBLIC_SALEOR_API_URL and SALEOR_TOKEN reach the store, then re-run jolly start --yes.
start.stock.check.stockSeeded.skipped: Cannot seed recipe stock: NEXT_PUBLIC_SALEOR_API_URL and/or SALEOR_TOKEN are not configured.
start.stock.check.stockSeeded.skipped.re: Complete the store stage so the endpoint and SALEOR_TOKEN are in .env, then re-run jolly start --yes.
start.stock.check.stockSeeded.pass: Seeded {DEFAULT_STOCK_QUANTITY} stock for {seededCount} recipe variant(s) in {warehouseSlug} via productVariantStocksCreate.
start.stock.check.stockSeeded.deployReci: Deploy the starter recipe with @saleor/configurator first, then re-run jolly start --yes.
start.stock.check.stockSeeded.endpointUn: Verify NEXT_PUBLIC_SALEOR_API_URL and SALEOR_TOKEN reach the store, then re-run jolly start --yes.
start.stripe.check.stripeAppInstalled.sk: Cannot install the Saleor Stripe app: NEXT_PUBLIC_SALEOR_API_URL and/or JOLLY_SALEOR_CLOUD_TOKEN are not configured.
Complete the store stage so the endpoint and Cloud token are available, then re-run jolly start --yes.
start.stripe.check.stripeAppInstalled.re: Reused the already-installed Saleor Stripe app (no duplicate installed).
start.stripe.check.stripeAppInstalled.in: Installed the Saleor Stripe app via Saleor GraphQL appInstall using the Cloud staff token.
start.stripe.check.stripeAppInstalled.re: Did not install the Saleor Stripe app: the appInstall request was rejected.
start.stripe.check.stripeAppInstalled.un: Did not install the Saleor Stripe app: the store could not be reached.
start.stripe.check.stripeAppInstalled.fa: Verify NEXT_PUBLIC_SALEOR_API_URL and JOLLY_SALEOR_CLOUD_TOKEN reach the store, then re-run jolly start --yes.
start.stripe.next: Open the installed Stripe app's configuration in the Saleor Dashboard, paste the publishable key and the Stripe restricted key, and map the configuration to the `us` channel (keys referenced by name only — Jolly does not perform this guided human gate).
start.storefront.check.storefrontPrepare: Reused the already-cloned storefront/ with installed dependencies (no re-clone).
Did not clone the storefront: storefront/ already exists but is not a Paper checkout.
Resolve the storefront/ directory collision (remove or rename it), then re-run jolly start --yes.
Did not clone the Saleor Paper storefront from main: {reason}.{stderr}
Verify `git` is installed and github.com is reachable, then re-run jolly start --yes.
Cloned Paper but could not initialize a fresh git repository: {error}.
Verify `git` is installed, then re-run jolly start --yes.
Cloned Paper but did not install dependencies: {reason}.{stderr}
Verify the npm registry is reachable (Jolly runs pnpm via `npx`), then re-run jolly start --yes.
Cloned saleor/storefront (Paper) from main into storefront/, initialized a fresh git repository, and installed dependencies with pnpm.
start.deploy.next: Hand this link to your human to open and approve the Vercel sign-in in their browser: {deviceUrl}. Tell them to reply "done" once they've approved; then re-run `{resumeCommand}` and I continue.
start.deploy.check.vercelDeployed.noStor: Cannot deploy: storefront/ is not prepared yet (no Paper checkout).
Complete the storefront stage so storefront/ exists, then re-run jolly start --yes.
start.deploy.check.vercelDeployed.noEndp: Cannot deploy: NEXT_PUBLIC_SALEOR_API_URL is not configured (complete the store stage first).
Complete the store stage so the endpoint is in .env, then re-run jolly start --yes.
start.deploy.check.vercelSignIn.pendingW: Vercel sign-in pending: Jolly runs the Vercel sign-in together with you via the official Vercel CLI under its own session (it holds no Vercel token). Approve the sign-in at the verification URL in nextSteps, then re-run jolly start --yes.
start.deploy.check.vercelSignIn.pending: Vercel sign-in pending: Jolly runs the Vercel sign-in together with you via the official Vercel CLI under its own session, and holds no Vercel token.
start.deploy.check.vercelDeployed.reason: Review the reported reason. Jolly runs the Vercel sign-in itself, so this is a deploy failure, not a sign-in step the agent must perform.
start.deploy.check.vercelDeployed.withUr: Deployed storefront/ to Vercel via the official Vercel CLI: {deployedUrl}
start.deploy.check.vercelDeployed.withou: Deployed storefront/ to Vercel via the official Vercel CLI (`npx vercel --prod`).
start.deploy.check.vercelDeploymentProte: Disabled Vercel Deployment Protection via the Vercel CLI; the storefront is publicly reachable.
Vercel Deployment Protection is on and could not be disabled automatically; disable it in the Vercel project settings so the store is publicly reachable.
start.deploy.check.storefrontLive.withUr: The deployed storefront ({deployedUrl}) is built against the Saleor Cloud endpoint and reaches Saleor Cloud (verified by a live GraphQL probe).
start.deploy.check.storefrontLive: The deployed storefront is built against the Saleor Cloud endpoint and reaches Saleor Cloud (verified by a live GraphQL probe).
start.deploy.check.storefrontLive.unreac: The deployed storefront ({deployedUrl}) is built against Saleor Cloud, but live connectivity to the endpoint could not be verified in this run.
The deployed storefront is built against Saleor Cloud, but live connectivity to the endpoint could not be verified in this run.
start.deploy.check.deployedStorefrontSer: The deployed storefront {deployedUrl} answers a live HTTP probe.
The deployed storefront {deployedUrl} did not answer within the readiness budget; the Vercel deployment may still be propagating. Re-run `jolly start` to resume.
start.deploy.check.vercelDeployed.cliExi: Did not deploy to Vercel: the Vercel CLI exited {status}.{stderr}
Review the Vercel CLI error above. Jolly runs the Vercel sign-in itself, so this is a deploy/build failure, not a sign-in step the agent must perform.
start.next.resolveBootstrapFailure: Resolve the bootstrap failure (see errors), then re-run jolly start.
start.next.approveStage: Approve the "{stage}" stage, then re-run jolly start to proceed.
start.next.resumeStages: Re-run jolly start to resume the remaining stages.
start.next.runLoginOrSetToken: Run `jolly login` to sign in through the Saleor device authorization grant, or set JOLLY_SALEOR_CLOUD_TOKEN for non-interactive use, then re-run jolly start.
start.next.keepBuilding: Your store is live. Keep building from what's on disk (after the restart above): `storefront/` is the Paper storefront (Next.js), live on Vercel — develop with `npx pnpm dev`, redeploy with `npx vercel`, and drive Paper-specific work from `storefront/AGENTS.md` and the embedded `saleor-paper-storefront` skill (in `storefront/skills/`), falling back to `storefront-builder` for generic patterns. `recipe.yml` is the catalog and config as code — edit it, preview with `npx @saleor/configurator diff`, then apply with `npx @saleor/configurator deploy --failOnDelete` (blocks a destructive apply over real catalog); the `saleor-configurator` skill owns the schema for new product types, attributes, and channels. To build a new Saleor app, the `saleor-app` skill covers the protocol — you already have staff auth (`SALEOR_TOKEN`, wired into `.mcp.json`), so install your finished app yourself with the Saleor GraphQL `appInstall` mutation, the same call Jolly makes for the Stripe app. Guides: https://github.com/saleor/storefront, https://github.com/saleor/configurator, https://docs.saleor.io.
start.next.interactiveGateFallback: If the agent cannot clear an interactive gate (such as new account creation), ask the human to run `jolly start` in a plain shell, then start their agent in that project to iterate (the skills jolly init installed are already on disk). This is a fallback — Jolly has not run it.
start.next.restartAgent: Restart your agent now, before anything else. The skills Jolly installed (the Jolly skill plus the Saleor/Stripe skills) only load on a fresh session, and these setup instructions will drift out of your context as you keep working — so restarting is required, not optional. Tell your human to restart you; after the restart the skills are active and the steps below will work.
start.summary.bootstrapFailed: Bootstrap failed; see errors. No downstream stage was performed.
start.summary.pausedForApproval: Bootstrap complete; paused for approval before the "{stage}" stage.
start.summary.blockedAtStage: Bootstrap complete; blocked at the "{firstBlockedStage}" stage — see nextSteps.
start.summary.proceeding: Bootstrap complete; proceeding through the orchestrated stages.
help.summary.success: Jolly — Ahoy, agent. Go build a store. (a tool by Dmytri Kleiner; not an official Saleor/Vercel/Stripe product)
help.next: Run jolly start to bootstrap setup and get the ordered playbook.
start.next.stageFailed: The {stage} stage failed. Fix the reported error, then re-run the stage.
start.summary: Ran the {stage} stage against its prepared preconditions: {status}.
cli.error.unknownCommand.remediation: Supported commands: login, logout, auth status, init, start, doctor, upgrade, skills, create, completion. Run `jolly help` for details.
cli.next.listCommands: List the Jolly commands and pick one.
createStore.env.error.unsupportedFlag.me: {bad} is not a recognized Jolly flag.
createStore.env.error.unsupportedFlag.re: Run `jolly <command> --help` to list the supported flags.
cli.next.listSupportedFlags: List the supported flags, then re-run without {bad}.
cli.error.unexpectedError.remediation: Re-run with --json and report the error code.
cli.next.reRunWithJson: Re-run the command with --json and report the error code in the envelope.
cloudError.remediation.environmentLimitR: Delete an unused environment or upgrade the plan, then re-run.
cloudError.remediation.domainLabelTaken: Choose a different domain label with --domain-label <label>.
cloudError.remediation.default: Confirm the Cloud token and that the Cloud API is reachable.
doctor.check.checkoutPaymentGateway.gate: Open the installed Stripe app's configuration in the Saleor Dashboard, paste the publishable and restricted keys, and map the configuration to the `us` channel.
start.summary.previewedPlan.storeSatisfi: Previewed the jolly start plan. The store stage is already satisfied (a store endpoint is configured), so no store would be created this run. No files were written and no network requests were made.
start.summary.previewedPlan: Previewed the jolly start plan. No files were written and no network requests were made.
start.recipe.check.recipeDeployed.notDep: the configurator could not be spawned
start.stock.check.stockSeeded.fail.recip: the starter recipe is not deployed yet (no recipe variants/warehouse to seed)
start.stock.check.stockSeeded.fail.endpo: the store could not be reached or the seeding request failed
start.deploy.check.vercelDeployed.reason: the Vercel CLI could not be spawned
